payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Pocket money task as compensated work unit
Weekly earnings summary as compensation record
Task claiming and completion tracking process
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Linkage between completed task and child
Weekly aggregation computes and presents earned amounts to children
Logical reporting interface aggregates child earnings and task completion data for parent view
Weekly cycle boundary for earned amount total access by child actors
Weekly task availability reset cycle timing

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task definition, assignment, claiming, and completion tracking process
payload.justification: Signal SG049 implies a strategic process through which children claim and track task completion. | Signal SG050 describes parental activity that represents a strategic process for defining compensated work.

payload.description: Compensation distribution process
payload.justification: Signal SG053 references accurate compensation distribution as a strategic process enabled by performance reporting.

payload.description: Pocket money task system including compensated work units and earnings records
payload.justification: Tasks are referenced across multiple signals as core things of strategic importance, representing the fundamental unit of work and compensation. | Signal SG052 identifies earnings summary as a strategically important thing that children need visibility into.

payload.description: Child performance report as accountability artifact
payload.justification: Signal SG053 establishes performance reporting as a thing of strategic importance for compensation decision-making.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history as analytical record
payload.justification: Signal SG054 identifies historical task data as a strategically important entity supporting accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task assignment
payload.justification: Signal SG050 explicitly references monetary values as an attribute of strategic importance in task definition.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle for task completion and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Signals SG049 and SG052 both reference weekly cadence as a strategic time dimension for task tracking and earnings visibility.

payload.description: Child stakeholder in pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as primary actors with distinct capabilities and needs in the enterprise context.

payload.description: Parent stakeholder in pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as key actors responsible for task definition and compensation distribution within the enterprise.

payload.description: Enable children to view, claim, and track available and completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG049 explicitly expresses a fundamental enterprise purpose at the strategic level, indicating a core goal of the pocket money system. | Signal SG051 captures strategic purpose around task transparency, representing a goal to inform and engage child stakeholders.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic goal, representing a key driver for the enterprise system.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Signal SG052 identifies a motivational goal at the strategic level, supporting stakeholder awareness and engagement.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG053 establishes a clear parental need as a strategic goal, linking performance visibility to compensation accuracy.

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access supporting accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal SG054 indicates a stakeholder requirement for historical data access, representing a strategic goal for accountability and insight generation.

payload.description: Child claiming completed task for monetary reward
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business process of children claiming completed tasks to track monetary rewards.

payload.description: Parent specification of task with monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 defines the business capability for parents to create and specify tasks with associated monetary values.

payload.description: Child viewing of available and completed tasks within timeframe
payload.justification: SG293 specifies the business capability for children to view both available and completed tasks within temporal scope.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of earned monetary totals
payload.justification: SG294 establishes the business capability to aggregate earnings on a weekly basis for child visibility.

payload.description: Parent reporting of child task earnings for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: SG295 defines the business capability for parents to view earnings reports to support payment activities.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundary
payload.justification: SG296 specifies the cyclical business rule governing when task availability is reset on a weekly basis.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: SG297 mandates the business rule requiring retention and accessibility of historical task information.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines task entities as business objects with monetary value attributes specified by parents.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute of task
payload.justification: SG291 specifies that tasks have associated monetary value as a defining attribute.

payload.description: Child entity participating in task completion
payload.justification: SG292 establishes child as a business entity linked to completed tasks.

payload.description: Association between child, completed task, and earnings for parent reporting
payload.justification: SG292 explicitly defines the relationship connecting completed task entities to child entities. | SG295 establishes the composite relationship between child, task, and earnings entities for business reporting purposes.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: SG292 and SG293 distinguish between available and completed task entities, implying completion status as a business attribute.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate for child
payload.justification: SG294 defines weekly aggregated monetary totals as a business data object visible to children.

payload.description: Historical task data across time periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates historical task data as retained business entities spanning temporal periods.

payload.description: Weekly time period for task and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG294 and SG296 establish the weekly cycle as the temporal boundary for earnings aggregation and task availability reset.

payload.description: Weekly boundary triggering task availability reset
payload.justification: SG296 defines the weekly temporal boundary as the business event that triggers task availability reset.

payload.description: Task completion event creating child-task linkage
payload.justification: SG290 and SG292 imply task completion as the business event that initiates the claim process and establishes the child-task relationship.

payload.description: Parent role specifying and managing tasks
payload.justification: SG291 and SG295 define parent as a business role with task specification and earnings reporting responsibilities.

payload.description: Child actor claiming tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: SG290, SG293, and SG294 establish child as a business actor who claims tasks, views task lists, and accesses earnings totals.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for children completing tasks
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the business objective of tracking monetary rewards through the task claim system.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment through earnings visibility
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly states the business objective of providing parent reporting to support payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to temporally scoped tasks
payload.justification: SG293 establishes the business constraint that child visibility of tasks is bounded by temporal scope.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible across time periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates the business constraint requiring ongoing accessibility of historical task information.

payload.description: Task completion events are logically linked to child entities through an association rule
payload.justification: SG457 describes a logical association rule defining the linkage between task completion events and child entities, clearly a How/Rule at the Designer level.

payload.description: Child users are granted logical access to task collections according to visibility requirements
payload.justification: SG458 specifies data visibility requirements and access patterns, which constitute logical access control rules governing how child users interact with task collections.

payload.description: System aggregates and presents child earnings and task completion data through weekly computation for display to children and reporting interface for parent view
payload.justification: SG459 describes temporal aggregation logic as a logical function that computes and presents weekly earned amounts, a clear How/Function at the Designer level. | SG460 describes a logical reporting interface that performs aggregation, which is a function that transforms and presents data to parents.

payload.description: Child entity
payload.justification: SG457 references child entities as logical data objects that participate in associations with task completion events.

payload.description: Task collection entity
payload.justification: SG458 references task collections as logical data entities subject to visibility and access pattern rules.

payload.description: Earned amount entity
payload.justification: SG459 references earned amounts as logical data entities that are computed and presented through temporal aggregation.

payload.description: Child earnings and task completion data entity
payload.justification: SG460 references child earnings as logical data entities included in the aggregated reporting view for parents. | SG460 references task completion data as a logical data entity that is aggregated and presented in the parent reporting interface.

payload.description: Historical task record entity
payload.justification: SG462 references historical task records as logical data entities subject to persistence and accessibility constraints.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governs task availability restoration through periodic state reset
payload.justification: SG461 explicitly describes weekly cycle behavior with periodic state reset logic, clearly a When/Cycle at the Designer level.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: SG457 explicitly references task completion events as logical state transitions that trigger association rule processing.

payload.description: Child user actor
payload.justification: SG458 and SG459 reference child users as logical actors who access task collections and receive presented earned amounts.

payload.description: Parent actor
payload.justification: SG460 references parents as logical actors who receive aggregated views of child earnings and task completion data.

payload.description: Historical task records must be logically persistent and accessible
payload.justification: SG462 mandates data retention requirements, which are design constraints governing the logical persistence and accessibility of historical records.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions for deployment, which represents a technical constraint on the system's deployment infrastructure at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly cycle timing for task availability reset and earned amount total access
payload.justification: SG542 establishes a weekly operational schedule boundary defining when specific data becomes accessible, representing detailed timing specification. | SG544 specifies the operational cycle timing for task availability reset operations, providing detailed sequencing specification.

payload.description: iOS deployment platform node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies three distinct named platform deployment nodes. iOS is the first named instance in this group. | SG545 identifies three distinct named platform deployment nodes. Android is the second named instance in this group. | SG545 identifies three distinct named platform deployment nodes. Windows is the third named instance in this group.

payload.description: Child actor executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 explicitly identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. Single actor type from single signal, not part of a named-instance group.

payload.description: Parent actor performing task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: SG540 explicitly identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task setup and monetary assignment. Single actor type from single signal, independently derived from SG539.

payload.description: Child role interface providing visibility of available and completed tasks within weekly cycles
payload.justification: SG541 defines operational visibility requirements specific to child actors, representing a detailed interface role specification at the implementer level.

payload.description: Parent role interface enabling report access for pocket money distribution
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role focused on accessing reports to execute distribution activities, representing a detailed interface specification.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
